pi_items.verified_value: [5, 12], with actual NREL 5MW optimal TSR of 7.55-7.82
pi_items.source: NREL technical documentation reports optimal TSR of 7.55 (at Cp=0.482) and 7.82 (at Cp=0.486), both within metadata range. Verified against NREL/TP-500-38060 and NREL forum discussions.

pi_items.verified_value: [0.3, 0.55], with actual NREL 5MW maximum Cp of 0.482-0.486
pi_items.source: NREL technical documentation reports Cp_max = 0.482-0.486 for NREL 5MW at TSR 7.55-7.82 with 0° blade pitch. Verified against NREL/TP-500-38060 and peer-reviewed analyses. README correctly notes Betz limit is 0.593 and typical modern turbines achieve 0.40-0.50.
Task specification and export_result.sh script. Verified through task.json and shell scripts that define output file locations.
Task specification and export_result.sh script. Verified through task.json and shell scripts that define output file locations.

Betz's law (1919, German physicist Albert Betz). Confirmed by Wikipedia, alternative energy tutorials, and multiple authoritative sources. Represents theoretical maximum power coefficient for any wind turbine.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the NREL 5MW reference wind turbine, a real, well-documented baseline design published by NREL (5-MW offshore, 126m rotor, 3-bladed). The turbine's verified aerodynamic performance: optimal Cp of 0.482-0.486 at TSR 7.55-7.82, which falls within the task's expected ranges of optimal_tsr_range [5,12] and max_cp_range [0.3,0.55]. The task correctly references the Betz limit of 0.593 (16/27) as the theoretical maximum. QBlade's BEM simulation with TSR sweep from 1 to 15 is a standard, realistic workflow for turbine performance evaluation. Data is not synthetic but based on NREL's published reference design available as sample projects in QBlade.
